title: Sampling Techniques & Sample Size Determination
text:
Overview Materi


1. Foundations
Mengapa sampling? Populasi vs. sampling frame. Sampling error vs. non-sampling error. Total Survey Error (TSE) framework. Bias-variance trade-off.
Mulai Belajar →


2. Probability Sampling
Simple Random Sampling, Systematic, Stratified, Cluster, dan PPS. Estimator, variance, dan perbandingan presisi antar metode.
Mulai Belajar →


3. Non-Probability Sampling
Convenience, purposive, quota, snowball, dan Respondent-Driven Sampling (RDS). Kapan justified dan bagaimana mitigasi bias.
Mulai Belajar →


4. Complex Designs
Multi-stage sampling, stratified cluster designs, Design Effect (DEFF), dan metode estimasi varians: TSL, BRR, JRR, Bootstrap.
Mulai Belajar →


5. Sample Size Determination
Dari formula dasar hingga power analysis, Bayesian sample size, dan pendekatan untuk machine learning. Interactive calculator included.
Mulai Belajar →


6. Weighting & DEFF
Base weight, non-response adjustment, calibration/raking, weight trimming, dan diagnostik. Effective sample size setelah weighting.
Mulai Belajar →


7. Practical Guide
Decision flowchart, checklist sebelum sampling, common mistakes, dan case study end-to-end: survei kepuasan pelanggan bank.
Mulai Belajar →

title: Sampling Techniques & Sample Size Determination
text:
Prerequisites






CatatanApa yang perlu dikuasai sebelum memulai?




Statistika dasar: distribusi, mean, varians, confidence interval, hypothesis testing.
R atau Python: kemampuan dasar manipulasi data dan visualisasi. Materi menyediakan code dalam kedua bahasa.
Konsep probabilitas: pemahaman dasar teori probabilitas dan distribusi sampling.
Linear algebra (opsional): membantu untuk memahami derivasi pada bab lanjut.









TipKapan teknik sampling penting vs. bisa diabaikan?



Sampling PENTING ketika:

Populasi terlalu besar/mahal untuk diteliti seluruhnya (sensus)
Pengujian bersifat destruktif (quality control)
Membutuhkan hasil cepat dengan sumber daya terbatas
Perlu estimasi parameter dengan presisi yang terukur

Sampling BISA DIABAIKAN ketika:

Populasi kecil dan mudah dijangkau → lakukan sensus
Data sudah tersedia secara lengkap (administrative data, log data)
Analisis big data di mana seluruh data bisa diproses
Tujuan hanya eksplorasi/deskriptif tanpa perlu inferensi ke populasi

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text: Survey weights menjembatani gap antara sampel dan populasi. Tanpa weighting yang benar, estimasi dari desain survei kompleks akan biased (Kish, 1992; Valliant dkk., 2018).

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text:
1 Base Weight

1.1 Definisi
Base weight = inverse of selection probability:
\[
w_i = \frac{1}{\pi_i}
\tag{1}\]
di mana \(\pi_i\) = probabilitas inklusi unit \(i\).
Untuk desain two-stage (PPS stage 1, SRS stage 2):
\[
w_i = \frac{1}{\pi_{i1} \times \pi_{i2|1}} = \frac{1}{n_1 \cdot p_i} \times \frac{N_{c(i)}}{n_{c(i)}}
\]
di mana \(p_i\) = probability cluster terpilih, \(N_{c(i)}\) = jumlah unit dalam cluster, \(n_{c(i)}\) = unit yang disampling.






CatatanSelf-Weighting Design



Jika semua unit memiliki base weight sama (\(w_i = w\) untuk semua \(i\)), desain disebut self-weighting. Ini terjadi ketika PPS digunakan di stage 1 dan equal-size SRS di stage 2. Self-weighting designs menghasilkan \(\text{DEFF}_w = 1\).




1.2 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Simulate two-stage design
dist_info <- pop |>
  summarise(N_d = n(), .by = district) |>
  mutate(prob_d = N_d / sum(N_d))

n_psu <- 15
m_ssu <- 20

# PPS selection of districts
cum_p <- cumsum(dist_info$prob_d)
int <- 1 / n_psu
st <- runif(1, 0, int)
pts <- st + (0:(n_psu - 1)) * int
sel_districts <- sapply(pts, function(p) {
  dist_info$district[min(which(cum_p >= p))]
})

# SRS within selected districts
weighted_sample <- pop |>
  filter(district %in% sel_districts) |>
  slice_sample(n = m_ssu, by = district) |>
  left_join(dist_info, by = "district") |>
  mutate(
    pi_1 = pmin(n_psu * prob_d, 0.999),
    pi_2 = m_ssu / N_d,
    pi_total = pi_1 * pi_2,
    base_weight = 1 / pi_total
  )

# Weight summary
weighted_sample |>
  summarise(
    min_wt = min(base_weight),
    median_wt = median(base_weight),
    mean_wt = mean(base_weight),
    max_wt = max(base_weight),
    cv_wt = sd(base_weight) / mean(base_weight),
    sum_wt = sum(base_weight)
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") |>
  mutate(Value = format(round(Value, 2), big.mark = ",")) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Base Weight Distribution Summary")

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text:
2 Non-Response Adjustment

2.1 Weighting Class Adjustment
Bagi sampel menjadi weighting classes (kelompok homogen terhadap response propensity). Dalam setiap class, inflate weight respondent untuk “mewakili” non-respondent:
\[
w_i^{NR} = w_i \times \frac{n_c}{n_c^{resp}}
\tag{2}\]
di mana \(n_c\) = total sampled in class \(c\), \(n_c^{resp}\) = respondents in class \(c\).


2.2 Propensity Model Adjustment
Model probabilitas response menggunakan logistic regression:
\[
\hat{p}_i = P(\text{respond} \mid X_i) = \text{logit}^{-1}(\beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{i1} + \ldots)
\tag{3}\]
Non-response adjusted weight: \(w_i^{NR} = w_i / \hat{p}_i\)

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Simulate non-response: higher income & urban less likely to respond
weighted_sample <- weighted_sample |>
  mutate(
    resp_prob = plogis(-0.5 + 0.3 * (urban_rural == "Rural") -
                         0.00000005 * income + 0.05 * household_size),
    responded = rbinom(n(), 1, resp_prob)
  )

response_rate <- mean(weighted_sample$responded)

# Method 1: Weighting class adjustment (by urban/rural × region)
nr_adj <- weighted_sample |>
  mutate(wt_class = paste(urban_rural, region)) |>
  mutate(
    n_class = n(),
    n_resp = sum(responded),
    nr_factor = n_class / n_resp,
    .by = wt_class
  ) |>
  mutate(
    wt_nr = ifelse(responded == 1, base_weight * nr_factor, NA)
  )

respondents <- nr_adj |> filter(responded == 1)

# Compare estimates
est_base <- weighted.mean(respondents$income, respondents$base_weight)
est_nr <- weighted.mean(respondents$income, respondents$wt_nr)
true_mean <- mean(pop$income)

tibble(
  Estimate = c("True Population Mean", "Respondents (base weight only)",
               "Respondents (NR adjusted)"),
  Mean_Income = c(true_mean, est_base, est_nr),
  Bias = c(0, est_base - true_mean, est_nr - true_mean)
) |>
  mutate(across(c(Mean_Income, Bias), ~format(round(.), big.mark = ","))) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title = "Non-Response Adjustment Effect",
    subtitle = sprintf("Response rate = %.1f%%", response_rate * 100)
  )

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text:
3 Calibration / Raking

3.1 Konsep
Calibration menyesuaikan weights agar distribusi marginal sampel match dengan known population totals dari sensus atau administrative data (Deville & Särndal, 1992).
Raking (iterative proportional fitting) menyesuaikan weights secara bergantian pada setiap variabel sampai konvergensi.


3.2 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
# Known population totals
pop_totals_urban <- pop |>
  count(urban_rural) |>
  mutate(Freq = n) |>
  select(urban_rural, Freq)

pop_totals_region <- pop |>
  count(region) |>
  mutate(Freq = n) |>
  select(region, Freq)

# Design with NR-adjusted weights
des_nr <- svydesign(
  id = ~district,
  weights = ~wt_nr,
  data = respondents
)

# Post-stratification on urban/rural
pop_table_urban <- xtabs(~urban_rural, data = pop)
obs_urban <- sort(unique(respondents$urban_rural))
pop_table_urban <- pop_table_urban[names(pop_table_urban) %in% obs_urban]
des_ps <- postStratify(des_nr, ~urban_rural, pop_table_urban, partial = TRUE)

# Raking on urban/rural AND region
pop_table_region <- xtabs(~region, data = pop)
obs_region <- sort(unique(respondents$region))
pop_table_region <- pop_table_region[names(pop_table_region) %in% obs_region]
des_rake <- rake(des_nr,
                  sample.margins = list(~urban_rural, ~region),
                  population.margins = list(pop_table_urban, pop_table_region))

# Compare
est_pre <- coef(svymean(~income, des_nr))
est_ps <- coef(svymean(~income, des_ps))
est_rake <- coef(svymean(~income, des_rake))

tibble(
  Method = c("True Mean", "Pre-calibration (NR adj only)",
             "Post-stratified (urban/rural)", "Raked (urban/rural + region)"),
  Estimate = c(true_mean, est_pre, est_ps, est_rake),
  Bias = c(0, est_pre - true_mean, est_ps - true_mean, est_rake - true_mean)
) |>
  mutate(across(c(Estimate, Bias), ~format(round(.), big.mark = ","))) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Calibration Effect on Estimates")

Distribusi weight sebelum dan sesudah calibration.

weight_compare <- tibble(
  weight = c(weights(des_nr), weights(des_rake)),
  stage = rep(c("Pre-calibration", "Post-raking"), each = nrow(respondents))
)

ggplot(weight_compare, aes(x = weight, fill = stage)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, alpha = 0.6, position = "identity") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pre-calibration" = "#d97706",
                                "Post-raking" = "#2563eb")) +
  labs(
    title = "Weight Distribution: Before vs After Raking",
    x = "Weight", y = "Count", fill = NULL
  )








Gambar 1: Distribusi weights sebelum dan sesudah raking.









Kode
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# Simplified raking demonstration in Python
sample_py = r.respondents.copy()
pop_py = r.pop.copy()

# Known population proportions
pop_urban_prop = (pop_py['urban_rural'] == 'Urban').mean()
pop_region_prop = pop_py['region'].value_counts(normalize=True).to_dict()

# Manual single-step calibration (urban/rural)
weights = sample_py['wt_nr'].values.copy()
urban_mask = (sample_py['urban_rural'] == 'Urban').values

# Adjust urban weights
current_urban_prop = weights[urban_mask].sum() / weights.sum()
urban_factor = pop_urban_prop / current_urban_prop
rural_factor = (1 - pop_urban_prop) / (1 - current_urban_prop)

cal_weights = weights.copy()
cal_weights[urban_mask] *= urban_factor
cal_weights[~urban_mask] *= rural_factor

# Compare
pre_mean = np.average(sample_py['income'], weights=weights)
post_mean = np.average(sample_py['income'], weights=cal_weights)
true_mean = pop_py['income'].mean()

results = pd.DataFrame({
    'Method': ['True Mean', 'Pre-calibration', 'Post-calibration (urban/rural)'],
    'Estimate': [f'{true_mean:,.0f}', f'{pre_mean:,.0f}', f'{post_mean:,.0f}'],
    'Bias': [0, f'{pre_mean - true_mean:,.0f}', f'{post_mean - true_mean:,.0f}']
})
results


                           Method Estimate   Bias
0                       True Mean  108,684      0
1                 Pre-calibration  110,052  1,368
2  Post-calibration (urban/rural)  111,543  2,860

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text:
4 Weight Trimming

4.1 Mengapa Trimming?
Extreme weights meningkatkan varians estimasi. Trimming meng-cap weights pada percentile tertentu (misalnya P95 atau P99) atau pada kelipatan dari median.
\[
w_i^{trim} = \min(w_i, w_{max})
\]






PeringatanTrade-off



Trimming mengurangi varians tapi memperkenalkan bias. Aturan praktis: trim jika \(\text{CV}(w) > 1.0\) atau jika ada weights yang >10× median (Potter, 1990).




4.2 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
wts <- weights(des_rake)

# Trimming at various percentiles
trim_levels <- c(0.95, 0.97, 0.99, 1.00)

trim_results <- map_dfr(trim_levels, function(p) {
  cap <- quantile(wts, p)
  trimmed <- pmin(wts, cap)
  est <- weighted.mean(respondents$income, trimmed)
  tibble(
    percentile = paste0("P", p * 100),
    cap_value = round(cap),
    mean_wt = mean(trimmed),
    cv_wt = sd(trimmed) / mean(trimmed),
    estimate = est,
    bias = est - true_mean
  )
})

trim_results |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~format(round(., 2), big.mark = ","))) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Weight Trimming: Effect on CV and Estimates")

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

wts = r.wts
cap_95 = np.percentile(wts, 95)
trimmed = np.minimum(wts, cap_95)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(9, 4), sharey=True)

axes[0].hist(wts, bins=40, alpha=0.6, color='#d97706')
axes[0].axvline(cap_95, linestyle='--', color='red')
axes[0].set_title('Original Weights')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Weight')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Count')

axes[1].hist(trimmed, bins=40, alpha=0.6, color='#2563eb')
axes[1].axvline(cap_95, linestyle='--', color='red')
axes[1].set_title('Trimmed (P95)')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Weight')

for ax in axes:
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(
        lambda x, p: f'{x:,.0f}'))

plt.suptitle(f'Weight Trimming (cap = {cap_95:,.0f})', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text:
5 Effective Sample Size Setelah Weighting
\[
n_{eff} = \frac{(\sum w_i)^2}{\sum w_i^2} = \frac{n}{1 + \text{CV}(w)^2}
\tag{4}\]

RPython




Kode
calc_neff <- function(w) {
  sum(w)^2 / sum(w^2)
}

tibble(
  Stage = c("Base weight", "NR adjusted", "Post-raking", "Trimmed (P95)"),
  n_actual = nrow(respondents),
  n_eff = c(
    calc_neff(respondents$base_weight),
    calc_neff(respondents$wt_nr),
    calc_neff(weights(des_rake)),
    calc_neff(trimmed_wts)
  ),
  CV_weights = c(
    sd(respondents$base_weight) / mean(respondents$base_weight),
    sd(respondents$wt_nr) / mean(respondents$wt_nr),
    sd(weights(des_rake)) / mean(weights(des_rake)),
    sd(trimmed_wts) / mean(trimmed_wts)
  ),
  DEFF_w = c(
    1 + (sd(respondents$base_weight) / mean(respondents$base_weight))^2,
    1 + (sd(respondents$wt_nr) / mean(respondents$wt_nr))^2,
    1 + (sd(weights(des_rake)) / mean(weights(des_rake)))^2,
    1 + (sd(trimmed_wts) / mean(trimmed_wts))^2
  )
) |>
  mutate(across(c(n_eff, CV_weights, DEFF_w), ~round(., 2))) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Effective Sample Size by Weighting Stage")

title: Survey Weighting & Adjustment
text:
6 Weight Diagnostics

6.1 Checklist Diagnostik

Distribusi weight: histogram, box plot — cari extreme values
CV of weights: target CV < 1.0; CV > 1.5 problematic
Sum of weights: harus ≈ \(N\) (population size)
Ratio max/min weight: jika > 20, pertimbangkan trimming
Marginal distributions: setelah calibration, distribusi variabel kunci harus match populasi


RPython




Kode
library(patchwork)

final_wts <- weights(des_rake)

# 1. Histogram
p1 <- ggplot(tibble(w = final_wts), aes(x = w)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "#2563eb", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(title = "Weight Distribution", x = "Weight", y = "Count")

# 2. Box plot by stratum
p2 <- respondents |>
  mutate(w = final_wts) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = region, y = w, fill = urban_rural)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sampling_colors) +
  labs(title = "Weights by Region × Urban/Rural", x = NULL, y = "Weight") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# 3. Marginal check: urban/rural
pop_prop <- prop.table(table(pop$urban_rural))
samp_unwt <- prop.table(table(respondents$urban_rural))
samp_wt <- prop.table(svytable(~urban_rural, des_rake))

marginal_df <- tibble(

Gambar 6: Weight diagnostics (Python).










6.2 Key Takeaways

Base weight = 1/π_i — ini adalah starting point. Jangan pernah analyze data survei tanpa weight.
Non-response adjustment mengoreksi bias dari unit yang tidak merespons, menggunakan weighting classes atau propensity models.
Calibration/raking menyesuaikan weight agar distribusi match dengan populasi yang diketahui — mengurangi bias dan bisa meningkatkan presisi.
Weight trimming mengurangi varians dari extreme weights, tapi trade-off dengan sedikit bias.
Selalu lakukan diagnostik: cek CV of weights, distribusi, dan marginal conformity setelah setiap tahap adjustment.



6.3 Exercises

Weight construction. Dari desain two-stage (15 district PPS, 20 RT per district), hitung base weight untuk setiap unit. Verifikasi bahwa \(\sum w_i \approx N\).
Non-response simulation. Simulasikan non-response yang berkorelasi dengan income (higher income = lower response). Bandingkan estimasi mean income: (a) tanpa adjustment, (b) dengan weighting class adjustment (urban/rural × education), (c) dengan propensity model. Mana yang paling efektif mengurangi bias?
Calibration exercise. Lakukan raking pada sampel menggunakan distribusi populasi: urban/rural, region, dan education level. Berapa persen pengurangan bias? Berapa n_eff setelah raking?



6.4 Further Reading

Valliant dkk. (2018): Panduan praktis pembuatan dan adjustment weights.
Deville & Särndal (1992): Teori formal calibration estimation.
Kish (1992): Konsep DEFF karena weighting dan implikasinya.

title: Complex Sampling Designs
text: Survei nasional jarang menggunakan SRS murni — mereka menerapkan stratified multi-stage cluster sampling yang mengkombinasikan beberapa teknik. Bab ini membahas bagaimana mendesain dan menganalisis survei kompleks tersebut (Heeringa dkk., 2017; Lumley, 2010).

title: Complex Sampling Designs
text:
1 Multi-Stage Sampling

1.1 Two-Stage Sampling

Stage 1 (PSU — Primary Sampling Unit): Pilih cluster dari populasi. Contoh: kecamatan.
Stage 2 (SSU — Secondary Sampling Unit): Dari PSU terpilih, pilih subsample. Contoh: rumah tangga.



1.2 Contoh: Desain Mirip SUSENAS
BPS SUSENAS menggunakan desain stratified two-stage:

Stratum: provinsi × urban/rural
PSU: blok sensus (dipilih PPS berdasarkan jumlah rumah tangga)
SSU: 10 rumah tangga per blok sensus (dipilih systematic)

1.3 Implementasi Two-Stage Design

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Stage 1: Select PSUs (districts) with PPS
district_info <- pop |>
  summarise(
    N_i = n(),
    mean_income = mean(income),
    .by = c(province, district, urban_rural)
  ) |>
  mutate(prob_pps = N_i / sum(N_i))

n_psu <- 15
m_ssu <- 20  # SSU per PSU

# PPS systematic selection
cum_prob <- cumsum(district_info$prob_pps)
interval <- 1 / n_psu
start <- runif(1, 0, interval)
select_pts <- start + (0:(n_psu - 1)) * interval

psu_selected <- sapply(select_pts, function(p) {
  district_info$district[min(which(cum_prob >= p))]
})

# Stage 2: SRS within each PSU
two_stage_sample <- pop |>
  filter(district %in% psu_selected) |>
  slice_sample(n = m_ssu, by = district)

# Setup survey design object
two_stage_sample <- two_stage_sample |>
  left_join(
    district_info |> select(district, N_i, prob_pps),
    by = "district"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    psu_prob = n_psu * prob_pps,
    ssu_prob = m_ssu / N_i,
    final_weight = 1 / (psu_prob * ssu_prob)
  )

# Survey design
des <- svydesign(
  id = ~district,
  strata = NULL,
  weights = ~final_weight,
  data = two_stage_sample,
  fpc = NULL
)

# Estimates
est_mean <- svymean(~income, des)
est_total <- svytotal(~income, des)

tibble(
  Parameter = c("Mean Income", "Total Income"),
  Estimate = c(
    format(round(coef(est_mean)), big.mark = ","),
    format(round(coef(est_total)), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  SE = c(
    format(round(SE(est_mean)), big.mark = ","),
    format(round(SE(est_total)), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `CI Lower` = c(
    format(round(confint(est_mean)[1]), big.mark = ","),
    format(round(confint(est_total)[1]), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `CI Upper` = c(
    format(round(confint(est_mean)[2]), big.mark = ","),
    format(round(confint(est_total)[2]), big.mark = ",")
  )
) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Two-Stage Design Estimates (survey package)")

Kode
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

np.random.seed(2024)
pop_py = r.pop.copy()

# Stage 1: Select districts
dist_info = pop_py.groupby('district').agg(
    N_i=('income', 'size'),
    mean_income=('income', 'mean')
).reset_index()
dist_info['prob'] = dist_info['N_i'] / dist_info['N_i'].sum()

n_psu = 15
m_ssu = 20

cum_prob = dist_info['prob'].cumsum().values
interval = 1 / n_psu
start = np.random.uniform(0, interval)
pts = start + np.arange(n_psu) * interval
psu_sel = [dist_info.iloc[np.searchsorted(cum_prob, p)]['district']
           for p in pts]

# Stage 2: SRS within PSU
samples = []
for d in psu_sel:
    d_data = pop_py[pop_py['district'] == d]
    s = d_data.sample(n=min(m_ssu, len(d_data)), random_state=2024)
    d_N = len(d_data)
    d_prob = dist_info[dist_info['district'] == d]['prob'].values[0]
    s = s.copy()
    s['weight'] = 1 / (n_psu * d_prob * m_ssu / d_N)
    samples.append(s)

sample_2s = pd.concat(samples)

# Weighted mean
weighted_mean = np.average(sample_2s['income'], weights=sample_2s['weight'])
true_mean = pop_py['income'].mean()

results = pd.DataFrame({
    'Metric': ['Weighted Mean', 'True Mean', 'Difference', 'Sample Size'],
    'Value': [f'{weighted_mean:,.0f}', f'{true_mean:,.0f}',
              f'{weighted_mean - true_mean:,.0f}', f'{len(sample_2s)}']
})

title: Complex Sampling Designs
text:
2 Stratified Multi-Stage Cluster Sampling
Desain survei nasional tipikal menggabungkan stratifikasi dengan multi-stage clustering:

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Create strata
pop <- pop |>
  mutate(stratum = paste(region, urban_rural, sep = "_"))

strata_list <- unique(pop$stratum)
n_psu_per_stratum <- 2
m_per_psu <- 15

# Stage 1: Select PSUs within each stratum
complex_sample_list <- list()
for (st in strata_list) {
  st_pop <- pop |> filter(stratum == st)
  st_districts <- unique(st_pop$district)

  if (length(st_districts) >= n_psu_per_stratum) {
    sel_psu <- sample(st_districts, n_psu_per_stratum)
  } else {
    sel_psu <- st_districts
  }

  # Stage 2: SRS within PSU
  st_sample <- st_pop |>
    filter(district %in% sel_psu) |>
    group_by(district) |>
    group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(m_per_psu, nrow(.x)))) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(
      stratum_id = st,
      psu_id = district,
      n_psu_in_stratum = length(sel_psu)
    )

  complex_sample_list[[st]] <- st_sample
}

complex_sample <- bind_rows(complex_sample_list)

# Compute weights (simplified)
complex_sample <- complex_sample |>
  left_join(
    pop |> summarise(N_h = n(), .by = stratum),
    by = c("stratum_id" = "stratum")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    base_weight = N_h / n(),
    .by = stratum_id
  )

# Survey design
des_complex <- svydesign(
  id = ~psu_id,
  strata = ~stratum_id,
  weights = ~base_weight,
  data = complex_sample,
  nest = TRUE
)

# Estimates
est_complex <- svymean(~income, des_complex)
est_by_region <- svyby(~income, ~region, des_complex, svymean)

est_by_region |>
  as_tibble() |>
  mutate(
    income = format(round(income), big.mark = ","),
    se = format(round(se), big.mark = ",")
  ) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Mean Income by Region (Complex Survey Design)")

title: Complex Sampling Designs
text:
3 Design Effect (DEFF) & Effective Sample Size

3.1 DEFF karena Clustering
\[
\text{DEFF}_{cl} = 1 + (\bar{m} - 1)\rho
\tag{1}\]
di mana \(\bar{m}\) = rata-rata unit per cluster, \(\rho\) = ICC.


3.2 DEFF karena Weighting
\[
\text{DEFF}_w = 1 + \text{CV}(w)^2
\tag{2}\]
di mana \(\text{CV}(w) = \text{SD}(w) / \text{mean}(w)\) adalah coefficient of variation dari weights.


3.3 Combined DEFF
\[
\text{DEFF} \approx \text{DEFF}_{cl} \times \text{DEFF}_w
\tag{3}\]


3.4 Effective Sample Size
\[
n_{eff} = \frac{n}{\text{DEFF}}
\tag{4}\]






PeringatanInterpretasi Praktis



Jika \(n = 1000\) dan \(\text{DEFF} = 2.5\), maka \(n_{eff} = 400\). Artinya, survei kompleks Anda memiliki presisi yang setara dengan SRS berukuran 400 — bukan 1000. Ini memiliki implikasi besar terhadap sample size planning.




3.5 Menghitung DEFF

title: Complex Sampling Designs
text:
4 Variance Estimation untuk Complex Designs
Untuk desain kompleks, variance tidak bisa dihitung dengan formula SRS sederhana. Empat metode utama:

4.1 Taylor Series Linearization (TSL)
Metode default di survey package. Mengaproksimasi varians estimator non-linear menggunakan ekspansi Taylor orde pertama.
\[
\hat{V}_{TSL}(\hat{\theta}) \approx \sum_{h=1}^H \frac{n_h}{n_h - 1} \sum_{i=1}^{n_h} (e_{hi} - \bar{e}_h)^2
\tag{5}\]
di mana \(e_{hi}\) = linearized residual dari PSU ke-\(i\) di stratum \(h\).


4.2 Replication Methods









Metode
Prinsip
Jumlah Replikasi
Cocok untuk




BRR
Half-sample replicates
\(n_h = 2\) per stratum required
Desain paired PSU


Jackknife (JRR)
Drop-one PSU
\(\sum n_h\) replikasi
General, robust


Bootstrap
Resample PSUs with replacement
B (biasanya 200-500)
Paling fleksibel





4.3 Implementasi Variance Estimation

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Design with paired PSUs for BRR compatibility
# Simplified: use 2 PSUs per stratum
strata_v <- unique(pop$region)
n_psu_v <- 2  # for BRR compatibility
m_v <- 30

var_sample_list <- list()
for (st in strata_v) {
  st_pop <- pop |> filter(region == st)
  dists <- unique(st_pop$district)
  sel <- sample(dists, min(n_psu_v, length(dists)))
  ss <- st_pop |>
    filter(district %in% sel) |>
    group_by(district) |>
    group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(m_v, nrow(.x)))) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(stratum_v = st, psu_v = district)
  var_sample_list[[st]] <- ss
}

var_sample <- bind_rows(var_sample_list) |>
  mutate(wt = N / n())

# TSL design
des_tsl <- svydesign(id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v,
                     weights = ~wt, data = var_sample, nest = TRUE)

# JRR design
des_jrr <- as.svrepdesign(des_tsl, type = "JKn")

# Bootstrap design
des_boot <- as.svrepdesign(des_tsl, type = "bootstrap", replicates = 200)

# Estimates comparison
tsl_est <- svymean(~income, des_tsl)
jrr_est <- svymean(~income, des_jrr)
boot_est <- svymean(~income, des_boot)

tibble(
  Method = c("TSL (Taylor)", "Jackknife (JKn)", "Bootstrap (B=200)"),
  Estimate = c(coef(tsl_est), coef(jrr_est), coef(boot_est)),
  SE = c(SE(tsl_est), SE(jrr_est), SE(boot_est))
) |>
  mutate(
    Estimate = format(round(Estimate), big.mark = ","),
    SE = format(round(SE), big.mark = ",")
  ) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title = "Variance Estimation Methods Comparison",
    subtitle = "Estimasi mean income — semua metode menghasilkan SE yang serupa"
  )

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# Simplified comparison using manual jackknife
sample_py = r.var_sample.copy()
clusters = sample_py['psu_v'].unique()
income_vals = sample_py['income'].values
weights = sample_py['wt'].values

# Weighted mean
theta_hat = np.average(income_vals, weights=weights)

# Jackknife: drop one cluster at a time
jack_estimates = []
for c in clusters:
    mask = sample_py['psu_v'] != c
    jack_est = np.average(income_vals[mask], weights=weights[mask])
    jack_estimates.append(jack_est)

jack_estimates = np.array(jack_estimates)
n_c = len(clusters)
jack_var = ((n_c - 1) / n_c) * np.sum((jack_estimates - theta_hat)**2)
jack_se = np.sqrt(jack_var)

results = pd.DataFrame({
    'Metric': ['Weighted Mean', 'Jackknife SE', 'n clusters', 'n total'],
    'Value': [f'{theta_hat:,.0f}', f'{jack_se:,.0f}',
              str(n_c), str(len(income_vals))]
})

5.6 Key Takeaways

Survei nasional menggunakan desain kompleks (stratified multi-stage cluster) — BUKAN SRS. Analisis harus memperhitungkan design features.
DEFF menghubungkan presisi aktual dengan presisi SRS. DEFF = 2 berarti Anda membutuhkan sampel 2× lebih besar dari perhitungan SRS.
Selalu gunakan svydesign() (atau as_survey_design()) di R. Jangan pernah ignore weights, PSU, atau strata.
Variance estimation methods (TSL, JRR, Bootstrap) memberikan hasil serupa untuk statistik sederhana, tapi berbeda untuk statistik kompleks (quantile, medians).
Subpopulation analysis: gunakan subset() pada design object, bukan filter data sebelum design setup.



5.7 Exercises

Design setup. Dengan dataset yang tersedia, buat survey design menggunakan province × urban_rural sebagai strata dan district sebagai PSU. Hitung mean income dan bandingkan SE dari svymean() vs. SE naif (tanpa design).
DEFF calculation. Hitung DEFF empiris untuk estimasi mean income pada desain di atas. Berapa effective sample size? Diskusikan implikasinya.
Regression comparison. Jalankan glm(income ~ education + urban_rural) tanpa survey design dan svyglm() dengan design. Bandingkan coefficients dan standard errors. Apa yang berubah dan mengapa?



5.8 Further Reading

Lumley (2010): Panduan komprehensif analisis survei kompleks di R.
Heeringa dkk. (2017): Pendekatan applied untuk analisis data survei.
Wolter (2007): Teori mendalam tentang estimasi varians.

title: Probability Sampling
text: Probability sampling adalah satu-satunya paradigma sampling yang memungkinkan inferensi berbasis desain (design-based inference) — setiap unit memiliki probabilitas seleksi yang diketahui dan >0, sehingga kita dapat mengkuantifikasi ketidakpastian estimasi secara formal (Lohr, 2021).

title: Probability Sampling
text:
1 Simple Random Sampling (SRS)

1.1 Konsep
Setiap subset berukuran \(n\) dari populasi \(N\) memiliki peluang yang sama untuk terpilih. Probabilitas inklusi setiap unit: \(\pi_i = n/N\).






CatatanSRS With vs. Without Replacement




SRSWR (with replacement): unit bisa terpilih >1 kali. Observasi independen. Jarang digunakan dalam survei.
SRSWOR (without replacement): unit hanya terpilih sekali. Standar dalam survei. Observasi tidak independen tetapi lebih efisien.





1.2 Estimator
Untuk SRSWOR:








Parameter
Estimator
Variance Estimator




Mean \(\bar{Y}\)
\(\bar{y} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n y_i\)
\(\hat{V}(\bar{y}) = (1-f)\frac{s^2}{n}\)


Total \(Y_{total}\)
\(\hat{Y} = N\bar{y}\)
\(\hat{V}(\hat{Y}) = N^2(1-f)\frac{s^2}{n}\)


Proporsi \(P\)
\(\hat{p} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n y_i\)
\(\hat{V}(\hat{p}) = (1-f)\frac{\hat{p}(1-\hat{p})}{n-1}\)



di mana \(f = n/N\) adalah sampling fraction dan \(s^2 = \frac{1}{n-1}\sum(y_i - \bar{y})^2\).


1.3 Finite Population Correction (FPC)
\[
\text{FPC} = 1 - f = 1 - \frac{n}{N}
\tag{1}\]


FPC signifikan ketika \(f > 0.05\) (sampling lebih dari 5% populasi). Untuk populasi besar atau sampling fraction kecil, FPC ≈ 1 dan bisa diabaikan.


1.4 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
n <- 200

# Draw SRS
srs_idx <- sample(1:N, n, replace = FALSE)
srs_sample <- pop[srs_idx, ]

# Estimates
srs_mean <- mean(srs_sample$income)
pop_mean <- mean(pop$income)
s2 <- var(srs_sample$income)
f <- n / N
se_srs <- sqrt((1 - f) * s2 / n)
ci_lower <- srs_mean - 1.96 * se_srs
ci_upper <- srs_mean + 1.96 * se_srs

# Plot
ggplot() +
  geom_histogram(data = pop, aes(x = income, y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 60, fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_histogram(data = srs_sample, aes(x = income, y = after_stat(density)),
                 bins = 40, fill = "#2563eb", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = pop_mean, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#dc2626", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = srs_mean, linetype = "solid",
             color = "#2563eb", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title = "SRS: Distribusi Sampel vs Populasi",
    subtitle = sprintf("n = %d | ȳ = %s | 95%% CI: [%s, %s] | μ = %s",
                       n, format(round(srs_mean), big.mark = ","),
                       format(round(ci_lower), big.mark = ","),
                       format(round(ci_upper), big.mark = ","),
                       format(round(pop_mean), big.mark = ",")),
    x = "Income", y = "Density"
  )

title: Probability Sampling
text:
2 Systematic Sampling

2.1 Konsep
Pilih unit setiap interval \(k = \lfloor N/n \rfloor\) setelah random start \(r \in [1, k]\). Sampel: unit ke-\(r, r+k, r+2k, \ldots\)


2.2 Risiko dan Mitigasi






PeringatanPeriodicity Danger



Jika frame memiliki pola periodik dengan periode = \(k\) (atau kelipatan), systematic sampling menghasilkan estimasi yang sangat biased. Contoh: daftar rumah tangga yang diurutkan per blok (setiap 10 rumah = 1 RT), lalu \(k = 10\) → selalu memilih posisi yang sama di setiap RT.



Mitigasi: (1) randomize urutan frame sebelum sampling, (2) gunakan circular systematic sampling, (3) multiple random starts.



2.3 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
n_sys <- 100
k <- floor(N / n_sys)
r <- sample(1:k, 1)

sys_idx <- seq(r, N, by = k)[1:n_sys]
srs_idx_100 <- sort(sample(1:N, n_sys))

plot_df <- tibble(
  position = c(sys_idx, srs_idx_100),
  method = rep(c("Systematic", "SRS"), each = n_sys)
)

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = position, y = method, color = method)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.15, size = 1.2, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("SRS" = "#2563eb", "Systematic" = "#059669")) +
  labs(
    title = "Posisi Unit Terpilih: SRS vs Systematic",
    subtitle = sprintf("n = %d, k = %d, random start r = %d", n_sys, k, r),
    x = "Position in Frame", y = NULL, color = "Method"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Probability Sampling
text:
4 Cluster Sampling

4.1 Konsep
Populasi dibagi menjadi cluster (kelompok, biasanya geografis). Pilih subset cluster secara random, lalu observasi semua unit dalam cluster terpilih (one-stage) atau subsample dalam cluster (two-stage).


4.2 Intra-class Correlation Coefficient (ICC)
\[
\rho = \frac{\sigma^2_b}{\sigma^2_b + \sigma^2_w}
\tag{4}\]
di mana \(\sigma^2_b\) = between-cluster variance, \(\sigma^2_w\) = within-cluster variance.


ICC tinggi → unit dalam cluster mirip satu sama lain → cluster sampling kurang efisien dibanding SRS. Idealnya, cluster harus heterogen secara internal (ICC rendah).


4.3 Design Effect (DEFF) karena Clustering
\[
\text{DEFF} = 1 + (m - 1)\rho
\tag{5}\]
di mana \(m\) = rata-rata jumlah unit per cluster. Jika \(\rho = 0.05\) dan \(m = 20\), maka \(\text{DEFF} = 1 + 19 \times 0.05 = 1.95\) — hampir dua kali lipat varians dibanding SRS!


4.4 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
districts <- unique(pop$district)
n_clusters <- 10

selected_clusters <- sample(districts, n_clusters)
cluster_sample <- pop |> filter(district %in% selected_clusters)

# Cluster-level summary
cluster_means <- cluster_sample |>
  summarise(
    mean_income = mean(income),
    n_units = n(),
    .by = district
  )

# Simple cluster estimator (equal-size assumption relaxed)
M <- length(districts)
m <- n_clusters
cluster_mean <- mean(cluster_means$mean_income)

ggplot(pop, aes(x = district, y = income)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = district %in% selected_clusters),
               outlier.size = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey85", "TRUE" = "#d97706")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Cluster Sampling: District sebagai Cluster",
    subtitle = sprintf("%d dari %d district terpilih | Total n = %d",
                       n_clusters, length(districts), nrow(cluster_sample)),
    x = NULL, y = "Income"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6))








Gambar 9: Cluster sampling: 10 dari 50 district dipilih, semua rumah tangga dalam cluster terpilih disurvei.







Kode
# Compute ICC using ANOVA approach
anova_fit <- aov(income ~ district, data = pop)
anova_tbl <- summary(anova_fit)[[1]]
msb <- anova_tbl$`Mean Sq`[1]
msw <- anova_tbl$`Mean Sq`[2]
n_bar <- nrow(pop) / length(districts)
icc <- (msb - msw) / (msb + (n_bar - 1) * msw)

tibble(
  Metric = c("ICC (ρ)", "Mean cluster size (m̄)", "DEFF = 1+(m̄-1)ρ",
             "Clusters selected", "Total sample size"),
  Value = c(round(icc, 4), round(n_bar, 1),
            round(1 + (n_bar - 1) * icc, 2),
            n_clusters, nrow(cluster_sample))
) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Cluster Sampling Metrics")

title: Probability Sampling
text:
5 Probability Proportional to Size (PPS)

5.1 Konsep
Unit dipilih dengan probabilitas proporsional terhadap ukuran (size measure) — misalnya populasi, jumlah rumah tangga, atau revenue. Unit besar memiliki peluang lebih tinggi untuk terpilih.


5.2 Estimator
Horvitz-Thompson estimator (Horvitz & Thompson, 1952):
\[
\hat{Y}_{HT} = \sum_{i \in s} \frac{y_i}{\pi_i}
\tag{6}\]
di mana \(\pi_i\) = probabilitas inklusi unit \(i\).
Hansen-Hurwitz estimator (PPS with replacement) (Hansen & Hurwitz, 1943):
\[
\hat{Y}_{HH} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \frac{y_i}{p_i}
\tag{7}\]
di mana \(p_i = X_i / X_{total}\) adalah probabilitas seleksi pada setiap draw.


5.3 Implementasi

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Size measure = number of households per district
district_size <- pop |>
  summarise(size = n(), total_income = sum(income), .by = district) |>
  mutate(prob = size / sum(size))

# PPS systematic selection of 10 districts
n_pps <- 10
cum_prob <- cumsum(district_size$prob)
interval <- 1 / n_pps
start <- runif(1, 0, interval)
select_points <- start + (0:(n_pps - 1)) * interval

pps_selected <- sapply(select_points, function(p) {
  district_size$district[min(which(cum_prob >= p))]
})

pps_sample <- pop |> filter(district %in% pps_selected)

# HT-like estimator for mean
pi_district <- district_size |>
  filter(district %in% pps_selected) |>
  mutate(pi_i = pmin(n_pps * prob, 1))

ggplot(district_size, aes(x = reorder(district, size), y = size)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = district %in% pps_selected)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey80", "TRUE" = "#dc2626"),
                    labels = c("Not selected", "Selected (PPS)")) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "PPS Sampling: Selection Proportional to District Size",
    subtitle = sprintf("%d districts selected via PPS systematic", n_pps),
    x = NULL, y = "Number of Households", fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6))

6 Perbandingan Metode: Simulasi Monte Carlo

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
B <- 1000
n_target <- 200
true_mean <- mean(pop$income)

# 1. SRS
srs_est <- replicate(B, {
  s <- slice_sample(pop, n = n_target)
  mean(s$income)
})

# 2. Stratified (proportional by region)
strat_est <- replicate(B, {
  s <- pop |> slice_sample(prop = n_target / N, by = region)
  est_h <- s |> summarise(ybar = mean(income), .by = region) |>
    left_join(strata_info |> select(region, W_h), by = "region")
  sum(est_h$W_h * est_h$ybar)
})

# 3. Cluster (10 random districts, all units)
cluster_est <- replicate(B, {
  sel <- sample(districts, 10)
  s <- pop |> filter(district %in% sel)
  mean(s$income)
})

# 4. Systematic
sys_est <- replicate(B, {
  k_sim <- floor(N / n_target)
  r_sim <- sample(1:k_sim, 1)
  idx <- seq(r_sim, N, by = k_sim)[1:n_target]
  idx <- idx[!is.na(idx)]
  mean(pop$income[idx])
})

# Summary
mc_summary <- tibble(
  Method = c("SRS", "Stratified", "Cluster", "Systematic"),
  Bias = c(mean(srs_est), mean(strat_est), mean(cluster_est),
           mean(sys_est)) - true_mean,
  Variance = c(var(srs_est), var(strat_est), var(cluster_est), var(sys_est)),
  MSE = c(mean((srs_est - true_mean)^2), mean((strat_est - true_mean)^2),
          mean((cluster_est - true_mean)^2), mean((sys_est - true_mean)^2)),
  RMSE = sqrt(c(mean((srs_est - true_mean)^2), mean((strat_est - true_mean)^2),
                mean((cluster_est - true_mean)^2), mean((sys_est - true_mean)^2)))
)

# Error bar plot
mc_long <- tibble(
  estimate = c(srs_est, strat_est, cluster_est, sys_est),
  method = rep(c("SRS", "Stratified", "Cluster", "Systematic"), each = B)
)

ggplot(mc_long, aes(x = method, y = estimate, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = true_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "red",
             linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sampling_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title = "Monte Carlo Comparison: Sampling Methods",
    subtitle = sprintf("B = %d simulations | True μ = %s | n ≈ %d",
                       B, format(round(true_mean), big.mark = ","), n_target),
    x = NULL, y = "Estimated Mean Income", fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Probability Sampling
text:
7 Tabel Ringkasan










Metode
Estimator Mean
Variance
Kapan Digunakan
DEFF vs SRS




SRS
\(\bar{y}\)
\((1-f)\frac{s^2}{n}\)
Frame lengkap, populasi homogen
1.0 (baseline)


Systematic
\(\bar{y}_{sys}\)
≈ SRS jika random order
Frame ordered, butuh simplicity
≈ 1.0


Stratified
\(\sum W_h \bar{y}_h\)
\(\sum W_h^2(1-f_h)\frac{s_h^2}{n_h}\)
Populasi heterogen, info auxiliary
< 1.0 (lebih efisien)


Cluster
\(\frac{1}{m}\sum \bar{y}_i\)
Depends on ICC
Constraint geografis/logistik
> 1.0 (kurang efisien)


PPS
\(\hat{Y}_{HT} = \sum \frac{y_i}{\pi_i}\)
HT variance
Cluster size varies, need proportional representation
Depends on correlation \(y\) & size




7.1 Key Takeaways

SRS adalah baseline — metode lain dibandingkan terhadapnya via Design Effect (DEFF).
Stratified sampling selalu lebih efisien (atau sama) dengan SRS jika stratifikasi variabel berkorelasi dengan variabel survei.
Cluster sampling mengorbankan presisi untuk efisiensi logistik. ICC dan cluster size menentukan seberapa besar “harga” yang dibayar.
PPS sampling tepat ketika cluster berukuran berbeda-beda — memberikan self-weighting design.
Pemilihan metode harus mempertimbangkan: ketersediaan frame, budget, variabilitas populasi, dan kebutuhan estimasi subgroup.



7.2 Exercises

Efficiency comparison. Dengan dataset populasi yang disediakan, bandingkan DEFF dari stratified sampling (stratum = urban_rural) vs. cluster sampling (cluster = district) untuk variabel expenditure. Mana yang lebih efisien dan mengapa?
Neyman vs. Proportional. Hitung alokasi Neyman untuk estimasi mean income menggunakan region sebagai stratum. Berapa persen efisiensi gain dibanding alokasi proporsional? Kapan perbedaannya substansial?
PPS application. Desain PPS sampling di mana Anda memilih 5 province dengan probabilitas proporsional terhadap jumlah rumah tangga, kemudian SRS 50 rumah tangga per province terpilih. Implementasikan dan bandingkan hasilnya dengan SRS \(n = 250\).



7.3 Further Reading

Cochran (1977), Chapters 2–5: Derivasi formal estimator dan varians untuk setiap metode.
Lohr (2021), Chapters 2–6: Pendekatan modern dengan contoh real-world.
Kish (1965), Chapter 4: Systematic sampling secara mendalam.

title: Fondasi Sampling
text:
Sampling bukan sekadar “mengambil sebagian data” — ia adalah framework inferensial yang memungkinkan kita membuat pernyataan tentang populasi berdasarkan subset yang jauh lebih kecil. Tiga argumen utama:

Biaya & waktu. Sensus Penduduk Indonesia (SP2020) membutuhkan anggaran triliunan rupiah dan ratusan ribu petugas. Survei sampel seperti SUSENAS mencakup ~300.000 rumah tangga dari ~70 juta — cukup untuk estimasi hingga level kabupaten.
Akurasi. Paradoks: sampel yang dirancang baik sering lebih akurat daripada sensus. Sensus rentan terhadap non-sampling error (pelatihan petugas yang tidak merata, kelelahan, data entry error) yang terakumulasi pada skala besar (Cochran, 1977).
Feasibility. Beberapa pengukuran bersifat destruktif (uji kualitas produk) atau populasi tidak terjangkau seluruhnya (hidden populations).







CatatanPrinsip Fundamental



Kualitas sampel ditentukan oleh desain, bukan hanya ukuran. Sampel 1.000 yang dirancang baik mengalahkan sampel 100.000 yang convenience-based — sebagaimana ditunjukkan oleh kegagalan Literary Digest poll 1936 (n = 2.4 juta) vs. keberhasilan Gallup poll (n = 50.000).

Gambar 1







2.2 Definisi

Target population: seluruh unit yang menjadi subjek inferensi. Contoh: seluruh rumah tangga di Indonesia.
Study population: subset target population yang secara praktis dapat dijangkau. Contoh: rumah tangga yang tinggal di wilayah yang aman dan accessible.
Sampling frame: daftar operasional dari mana sampel ditarik. Contoh: daftar blok sensus SP2020 (BPS), daftar nasabah aktif bank, database nomor telepon.



2.3 Coverage Error
Coverage error = perbedaan antara target population dan sampling frame:

Undercoverage: unit di populasi tidak ada di frame. Contoh: rumah tangga baru yang belum tercatat di SP2020; pelanggan baru yang belum masuk database.
Overcoverage: unit di frame bukan bagian dari populasi. Contoh: rumah tangga yang sudah pindah/meninggal tapi masih tercatat.
Duplikasi: unit muncul >1 kali di frame.







PeringatanPitfall



Coverage error adalah salah satu sumber bias paling berbahaya karena tidak bisa dideteksi dari data sampel — kita tidak tahu apa yang tidak kita ketahui. Selalu evaluasi kualitas frame sebelum mendesain survei.

title: Fondasi Sampling
text:
3 Konsep Kunci

3.1 Sampling Error vs. Non-Sampling Error


Sampling error menurun dengan bertambahnya \(n\). Non-sampling error bisa meningkat seiring bertambahnya \(n\) jika quality control memburuk.
Sampling error adalah variabilitas estimasi yang timbul karena kita hanya mengobservasi sebagian populasi. Jika kita mengambil sampel berbeda, kita mendapat estimasi berbeda — variabilitas inilah sampling error.
Non-sampling error mencakup semua error lainnya:

Measurement error: responden salah paham pertanyaan
Processing error: salah input data
Non-response error: responden menolak/tidak bisa dihubungi
Coverage error: frame tidak mencakup populasi



3.2 Bias vs. Variance Trade-off
Untuk estimator \(\hat{\theta}\) dari parameter \(\theta\):
\[
\text{MSE}(\hat{\theta}) = \text{Bias}^2(\hat{\theta}) + \text{Var}(\hat{\theta})
\tag{1}\]
di mana:

\(\text{Bias}(\hat{\theta}) = E[\hat{\theta}] - \theta\)
\(\text{Var}(\hat{\theta}) = E[(\hat{\theta} - E[\hat{\theta}])^2]\)

Estimator yang unbiased memiliki \(E[\hat{\theta}] = \theta\), sehingga \(\text{MSE} = \text{Var}\). Namun dalam praktik, sedikit bias kadang bisa diterima jika menghasilkan penurunan varians yang substansial.


3.3 Parameter, Estimator, Estimate








Konsep
Definisi
Contoh




Parameter (\(\theta\))
Nilai tetap (unknown) di populasi
\(\bar{Y} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N Y_i\)


Estimator (\(\hat{\theta}\))
Fungsi dari data sampel (random variable)
\(\bar{y} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n y_i\)


Estimate
Nilai numerik spesifik dari estimator
\(\bar{y} = 4{,}250{,}000\)





3.4 Margin of Error dan Confidence Interval
Margin of Error (MoE) pada level kepercayaan \((1-\alpha)\):
\[
\text{MoE} = z_{1-\alpha/2} \times \text{SE}(\hat{\theta})
\tag{2}\]
Confidence Interval:
\[
\text{CI}_{1-\alpha} = \hat{\theta} \pm \text{MoE}
\tag{3}\]






TipInterpretasi yang Benar



CI 95% bukan berarti “ada probabilitas 95% parameter ada di interval ini.” Interpretasi yang benar: jika kita mengulangi prosedur sampling berkali-kali, 95% dari interval yang dihasilkan akan mengandung parameter sebenarnya.

4 Total Survey Error (TSE) Framework
TSE framework (Groves dkk., 2009) mengorganisir semua sumber error dalam survei menjadi dua dimensi:

    subgraph Representation["<b>Representation</b>"]

        direction TB

Point penting: Sampling error (yang bisa dihitung dari data) hanyalah satu komponen kecil dari total error. Fokus berlebihan pada sampling error (memperbesar \(n\)) sementara mengabaikan non-sampling error adalah kesalahan umum.

title: Fondasi Sampling
section: 5 Simulasi: Central Limit Theorem dan Sampling Distribution
text:
5 Simulasi: Central Limit Theorem dan Sampling Distribution
Kita akan mendemonstrasikan bahwa distribusi sampling mean mendekati normal seiring bertambahnya \(n\), bahkan ketika distribusi populasi sangat skewed.

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
true_mean <- mean(pop$income)
sample_sizes <- c(10, 30, 100, 500)
B <- 2000

sim_results <- map_dfr(sample_sizes, function(n) {
  means <- replicate(B, mean(sample(pop$income, n)))
  tibble(n = paste0("n = ", n), sample_mean = means)
}) |>
  mutate(n = factor(n, levels = paste0("n = ", sample_sizes)))

ggplot(sim_results, aes(x = sample_mean)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 50,
                 fill = "#2563eb", alpha = 0.6, color = "white") +
  geom_density(color = "#dc2626", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = true_mean, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#059669", linewidth = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~n, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Central Limit Theorem: Distribusi Sampling Mean",
    subtitle = paste0("Populasi: income (N = ", nrow(pop),
                      ", μ = ", format(round(true_mean), big.mark = ","), ")"),
    x = "Sample Mean (Income)",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  annotate("text", x = Inf, y = Inf, label = "— True μ",
           hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.5, color = "#059669", size = 3.5)








Gambar 3: Distribusi sampling mean untuk berbagai ukuran sampel. Populasi income berdistribusi right-skewed (log-normal), namun distribusi \(\bar{y}\) mendekati normal seiring bertambahnya n.









Kode
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

np.random.seed(2024)

pop_py = r.pop  # access R dataframe
income = pop_py['income'].values
true_mean = income.mean()

sample_sizes = [10, 30, 100, 500]
B = 2000

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(10, 7))
axes = axes.flatten()

for idx, n in enumerate(sample_sizes):
    means = [np.mean(np.random.choice(income, size=n, replace=False))
             for _ in range(B)]
    ax = axes[idx]
    ax.hist(means, bins=50, density=True, alpha=0.6, color='#2563eb',
            edgecolor='white')
    ax.axvline(true_mean, linestyle='--', color='#059669', linewidth=1.2,
               label=f'True μ = {true_mean:,.0f}')
    ax.set_title(f'n = {n}', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Sample Mean (Income)')
    ax.set_ylabel('Density')
    ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(
        lambda x, p: f'{x:,.0f}'))
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)

fig.suptitle('Central Limit Theorem: Distribusi Sampling Mean',
             fontweight='bold', fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Gambar 4: Distribusi sampling mean untuk berbagai ukuran sampel (Python implementation).

title: Fondasi Sampling
text:
6 Sampling Error vs. Sample Size
Hubungan antara sampling error dan ukuran sampel bersifat diminishing returns — peningkatan presisi melambat seiring bertambahnya \(n\).

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
n_values <- c(10, 20, 30, 50, 100, 200, 300, 500, 750, 1000)
B <- 2000

se_sim <- map_dfr(n_values, function(n) {
  means <- replicate(B, mean(sample(pop$income, n)))
  tibble(n = n, se_empirical = sd(means))
})

pop_sd <- sd(pop$income)
se_sim <- se_sim |>
  mutate(se_theoretical = pop_sd / sqrt(n))

ggplot(se_sim, aes(x = n)) +
  geom_point(aes(y = se_empirical, color = "Empirical (simulasi)"),
             size = 3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = se_theoretical, color = "Theoretical (σ/√n)"),
            linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Empirical (simulasi)" = "#2563eb",
                                "Theoretical (σ/√n)" = "#dc2626")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = n_values) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title = "Standard Error vs. Sample Size",
    subtitle = "SE menurun proporsional dengan 1/√n — diminishing returns",
    x = "Sample Size (n)",
    y = "Standard Error of Mean",
    color = NULL
  )








Gambar 5: Standard Error menurun proporsional dengan \(1/\sqrt{n}\). Perhatikan diminishing returns: dari n=30 ke n=100 jauh lebih impactful daripada dari n=500 ke n=1000.









Kode
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

np.random.seed(2024)

income = r.pop['income'].values
pop_sd = income.std(ddof=0)

n_values = [10, 20, 30, 50, 100, 200, 300, 500, 750, 1000]
B = 2000

se_empirical = []
for n in n_values:
    means = [np.mean(np.random.choice(income, size=n, replace=False))
             for _ in range(B)]
    se_empirical.append(np.std(means, ddof=1))

se_theoretical = [pop_sd / np.sqrt(n) for n in n_values]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
ax.scatter(n_values, se_empirical, color='#2563eb', s=60, zorder=5,
           label='Empirical (simulasi)')
ax.plot(n_values, se_theoretical, color='#dc2626', linewidth=1.5,
        label='Theoretical (σ/√n)')
ax.set_xlabel('Sample Size (n)')
ax.set_ylabel('Standard Error of Mean')
ax.set_title('Standard Error vs. Sample Size', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend()
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(
    lambda x, p: f'{x:,.0f}'))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Gambar 6: Standard Error vs. Sample Size (Python implementation).














CatatanImplikasi Praktis



Menggandakan presisi (mengurangi SE menjadi setengah) membutuhkan empat kali lipat ukuran sampel (\(n \propto 1/\text{SE}^2\)). Ini adalah alasan utama mengapa penentuan ukuran sampel harus berbasis perhitungan — bukan sekadar “ambil sebanyak mungkin.”



6.1 Key Takeaways

Kualitas sampling ditentukan oleh desain, bukan hanya ukuran. Sampel kecil yang well-designed mengalahkan sampel besar yang biased.
Sampling error hanyalah satu komponen dari Total Survey Error. Non-sampling error (coverage, non-response, measurement) sering lebih besar dan lebih sulit dikendalikan.
Sampling frame adalah titik kritis — gap antara frame dan target population menghasilkan coverage error yang tidak terdeteksi dari data.
Standard error menurun proporsional dengan \(1/\sqrt{n}\) — ada diminishing returns. Perhitungan formal sample size diperlukan untuk menentukan titik optimal.
CLT menjamin distribusi sampling mean mendekati normal untuk \(n\) yang cukup besar, memungkinkan konstruksi CI dan hypothesis testing.



6.2 Exercises

Coverage error analysis. Anda diminta mendesain survei kepuasan pelanggan e-commerce. Frame yang tersedia: database email pelanggan yang pernah bertransaksi dalam 12 bulan terakhir. Identifikasi potensi undercoverage dan overcoverage. Bagaimana dampaknya terhadap estimasi, dan apa mitigasinya?
Bias-variance simulation. Modifikasi simulasi CLT di atas: alih-alih SRS, gunakan sampling yang hanya mengambil rumah tangga urban. Hitung bias dan MSE dari estimator mean income, dan bandingkan dengan SRS. Apa pelajarannya?
TSE decomposition. Sebuah survei telepon memiliki response rate 40%. Diskusikan komponen TSE mana saja yang kemungkinan besar berkontribusi pada total error, dan urutkan dari yang paling berpengaruh.



6.3 Further Reading

Groves dkk. (2009), Chapter 2: Total Survey Error framework secara lengkap.
Lohr (2021), Chapter 1-2: Fondasi sampling dengan contoh modern.
Cochran (1977), Chapter 1: Argumen klasik tentang mengapa sampling bekerja.

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text: Non-probability sampling terjadi ketika probabilitas inklusi unit tidak diketahui atau nol untuk sebagian populasi. Meski tidak memungkinkan inferensi berbasis desain klasik, metode ini sering menjadi satu-satunya opsi yang feasible — dan pendekatan modern memungkinkan mitigasi bias melalui model-based adjustment (Elliott & Valliant, 2017).

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text:
1 Convenience Sampling

1.1 Definisi & Mekanisme
Sampel diambil dari unit yang paling mudah diakses: pengunjung mall, mahasiswa di kelas, follower media sosial, responden panel online sukarela.


1.2 Bias yang Muncul

Selection bias: unit yang mudah diakses sistematik berbeda dari populasi
Self-selection bias: yang bersedia berpartisipasi berbeda dari yang menolak
Coverage bias: hanya menjangkau subpopulasi tertentu



1.3 Kapan Justified?

Pilot testing: menguji instrumen survei sebelum survei formal
Exploratory research: generating hypotheses, bukan estimasi parameter
UX testing: usability test di mana generalizability bukan tujuan utama
Resource constraints: ketika budget/waktu sangat terbatas dan awareness bias didokumentasikan







PeringatanPitfall Umum



Convenience sampling tidak menjadi lebih valid hanya karena ukuran sampel besar. Sampel 100.000 dari pengunjung website tetap biased jika populasi target adalah seluruh pelanggan — termasuk yang tidak online.

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text:
2 Purposive / Judgmental Sampling

2.1 Definisi & Mekanisme
Peneliti secara sengaja memilih unit berdasarkan pengetahuan/judgment tentang populasi. Variasi:

Expert sampling: memilih informan kunci yang paling berpengetahuan
Maximum variation: memilih kasus yang paling berbeda untuk memahami range fenomena
Homogeneous sampling: memilih kasus serupa untuk studi mendalam
Typical case sampling: memilih kasus “rata-rata” atau tipikal



2.2 Kapan Justified?

Qualitative research: di mana depth > breadth
Rare populations: ketika frame tidak tersedia
Policy evaluation: site selection untuk program evaluation
Mixed methods: complementing probability survey dengan case studies

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text:
3 Quota Sampling

3.1 Definisi & Mekanisme
Populasi dibagi ke dalam kategori (quota), lalu interviewer diminta mengisi kuota masing-masing. Bedanya dengan stratified sampling: cara pemilihan unit dalam kuota bersifat non-random (discretion pewawancara).



Aspek
Stratified Sampling
Quota Sampling




Pembagian strata/kuota
✅
✅


Random selection dalam strata
✅
❌


Known \(\pi_i\)
✅
❌


Kontrol distribusi variabel
✅
Parsial


Potensi selection bias
Minimal
Signifikan





3.2 Contoh Studi
Market research menggunakan quota: “Interview 200 orang, pastikan 50% pria, 30% usia <30, 20% usia >50.” Pewawancara memilih siapa yang diwawancara — memperkenalkan bias (cenderung memilih yang kooperatif, accessible, non-threatening).

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text:
4 Snowball Sampling

4.1 Definisi & Mekanisme
Mulai dari seed/initial respondents, minta mereka merujuk orang lain yang memenuhi kriteria. Proses berlanjut iteratif (wave/chain).


4.2 Kapan Satu-satunya Opsi?
Hidden populations — populasi yang sulit dijangkau melalui frame konvensional:

Pengguna narkoba (UNODC surveys)
Sex workers (HIV surveillance)
Undocumented migrants
Rare disease patients
Underground communities



4.3 Bias yang Muncul

Initial seed bias: komposisi sampel sangat dipengaruhi oleh seed awal
Network homophily: orang cenderung merujuk yang mirip diri sendiri
Voluntarism bias: yang bersedia berpartisipasi dan merujuk berbeda dari yang menolak
Masking: beberapa subgroup mungkin tidak terjangkau karena terisolasi dari jaringan utama

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text:
5 Respondent-Driven Sampling (RDS)

5.1 Konsep
RDS (Heckathorn, 1997, 2002) adalah “improved snowball” yang menambahkan basis probabilistik:

Seed yang dipilih secara purposive memulai chain
Setiap responden hanya boleh merujuk sejumlah tetap (biasanya 3) orang
Dual incentive: kompensasi untuk berpartisipasi DAN untuk setiap referral yang berhasil
Data tentang network size (derajat) dikumpulkan dari setiap responden
Estimator RDS meng-adjust untuk network size → approximation terhadap probability sampling



5.2 RDS Estimator
\[
\hat{\mu}_{RDS-II} = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^n y_i / d_i}{\sum_{i=1}^n 1/d_i}
\tag{1}\]
di mana \(d_i\) = network size (derajat) responden \(i\). Inverse network size berperan sebagai weight — responden dengan jaringan besar diberi bobot lebih kecil karena mereka over-represented.






CatatanAsumsi RDS




Populasi membentuk satu komponen jaringan terhubung
Sampling with replacement (atau populasi cukup besar)
Random recruitment dari network masing-masing
Responden melaporkan network size dengan akurat
Chain cukup panjang untuk mencapai equilibrium (convergence)

Pelanggaran asumsi ini (terutama #3 dan #5) sering terjadi di lapangan, menghasilkan bias substansial.

title: Non-Probability Sampling
section: 6 Simulasi: Bias dari Convenience Sampling vs. SRS
text:
6 Simulasi: Bias dari Convenience Sampling vs. SRS

RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)
B <- 1000
n_sim <- 200
true_mean <- mean(pop$income)

# SRS
srs_est <- replicate(B, mean(sample(pop$income, n_sim)))

# Convenience: 80% urban, 20% rural (vs population ~50/50)
conv_est <- replicate(B, {
  n_urban <- round(n_sim * 0.80)
  n_rural <- n_sim - n_urban
  urban_samp <- sample(pop$income[pop$urban_rural == "Urban"], n_urban)
  rural_samp <- sample(pop$income[pop$urban_rural == "Rural"], n_rural)
  mean(c(urban_samp, rural_samp))
})

# Convenience: only high education
conv_edu_est <- replicate(B, {
  eligible <- pop |> filter(education %in% c("S1", "S2+"))
  mean(sample(eligible$income, min(n_sim, nrow(eligible))))
})

sim_df <- tibble(
  estimate = c(srs_est, conv_est, conv_edu_est),
  method = rep(c("SRS (unbiased)",
                 "Convenience (urban-biased)",
                 "Convenience (edu-biased)"), each = B)
)

ggplot(sim_df, aes(x = estimate, fill = method)) +

title: Non-Probability Sampling
text:
8 Debat Modern: Non-Probability + Model-Based Inference

8.1 Kapan Non-Probability Bisa “Diselamatkan”?
Pendekatan modern (Elliott & Valliant, 2017; Wang dkk., 2015) menggunakan model-based adjustment untuk mengurangi bias dari non-probability samples:

Propensity Score Adjustment: Model probabilitas masuk ke sampel non-probability (\(P(\text{in sample} | X)\)) menggunakan auxiliary data. Inverse propensity score digunakan sebagai weight.
Calibration Weighting: Sesuaikan distribusi sampel agar match dengan distribusi populasi yang diketahui (dari sensus atau administrative data) pada variabel-variabel kunci.
Doubly Robust Estimation: Kombinasi propensity model dan outcome model — konsisten jika salah satu model benar.
Small Area Estimation: Gunakan model multilevel untuk “meminjam kekuatan” dari auxiliary data dan link non-probability sample ke populasi.







TipSyarat Keberhasilan



Model-based adjustment untuk non-probability sample berhasil jika:

Auxiliary variables (\(X\)) yang tersedia menjelaskan perbedaan antara sample dan populasi
Asumsi ignorability terpenuhi: setelah conditioning on \(X\), selection independent of \(Y\)
Population benchmarks (distribusi \(X\) di populasi) tersedia dan reliable
Variasi dalam propensity score cukup — tidak ada strata populasi yang sama sekali tidak ter-cover

Jika asumsi dilanggar, adjustment justru bisa memperburuk bias.




8.2 Framework Keputusan

9 Ringkasan Metode Non-Probability










Metode
Mekanisme
Bias Utama

Purposive
Peneliti memilih
Expert judgment bias
Qualitative, rare populations
Terbatas


Quota
Kuota per kategori, non-random fill
Selection within quotas
Market research, quick estimates
Post-stratification


Snowball
Referral chains
Seed bias, homophily
Hidden populations
RDS adjustment


RDS
Structured snowball + incentive
Assumptions often violated
Hidden populations (improved)
Network-size weighting




9.1 Key Takeaways

Non-probability ≠ invalid. Konteks menentukan. Untuk hidden populations atau exploratory research, non-probability sering justified.
Bias ≠ hanya masalah ukuran. Memperbesar \(n\) pada convenience sampling tidak mengurangi bias — hanya mengurangi variance.
Model-based adjustment memungkinkan inferensi dari non-probability samples, dengan syarat auxiliary data kuat dan asumsi terpenuhi.
RDS bukan silver bullet. Asumsinya sering dilanggar; hasil RDS harus diinterpretasi dengan hati-hati dan sensitivity analysis.
Transparansi adalah kunci. Jika menggunakan non-probability, dokumentasikan mekanisme seleksi, potensi bias, dan batasan inferensi.



9.2 Exercises

Bias quantification. Sebuah survei online tentang kepuasan pelanggan bank mendapat 5.000 responden. Data populasi menunjukkan 60% nasabah berusia >40 tahun, tapi hanya 25% responden survei berusia >40. Hitung potensi bias menggunakan simulasi jika kepuasan berkorelasi positif dengan usia.
RDS design. Anda diminta mendesain RDS untuk mengestimasi prevalensi penggunaan narkoba di kota besar. Jelaskan: (a) berapa seed yang diperlukan, (b) berapa coupon per responden, (c) bagaimana mengukur network size, (d) kapan chain dianggap converged.
Adjustment exercise. Gunakan dataset simulasi: tarik convenience sample yang over-represents urban + high education. Lakukan post-stratification menggunakan distribusi urban_rural × education dari populasi. Bandingkan estimasi sebelum dan sesudah adjustment.



9.3 Further Reading

Elliott & Valliant (2017): Framework komprehensif untuk inferensi dari non-probability samples.
Wang dkk. (2015): Studi kasus forecasting election dengan non-representative polls.
Heckathorn (2002): Teori formal RDS dengan derivasi estimator.

title: Penentuan Ukuran Sampel
text: Penentuan ukuran sampel (sample size determination) adalah keputusan desain paling kritis — terlalu kecil menghasilkan presisi rendah, terlalu besar membuang sumber daya. Bab ini menyajikan pendekatan dari yang paling sederhana hingga advanced (Cochran, 1977; Cohen, 1988; Lohr, 2021).

title: Penentuan Ukuran Sampel
text:
1 SRS untuk Estimasi Mean

1.1 Formula Dasar
Untuk mengestimasi population mean \(\bar{Y}\) dengan margin of error \(e\) pada confidence level \((1-\alpha)\):
\[
n = \frac{z^2_{1-\alpha/2} \cdot \sigma^2}{e^2}
\tag{1}\]
Dengan Finite Population Correction (FPC):
\[
n = \frac{n_0}{1 + n_0/N} \quad \text{di mana } n_0 = \frac{z^2 \sigma^2}{e^2}
\tag{2}\]


\(\sigma^2\) biasanya tidak diketahui. Gunakan: (1) pilot study, (2) data sebelumnya, (3) range/4 rule: \(\sigma \approx (\max - \min)/4\), atau (4) conservative estimate.


1.2 Contoh Numerik
Ingin mengestimasi mean income dengan $e = $ Rp 500.000, $= $ Rp 3.000.000, 95% confidence:
\[
n_0 = \frac{1.96^2 \times 3{,}000{,}000^2}{500{,}000^2} = \frac{3.8416 \times 9 \times 10^{12}}{2.5 \times 10^{11}} = 138.3 \approx 139
\]
Dengan FPC (\(N = 10{,}000\)): \(n = 139 / (1 + 139/10000) = 137.1 \approx 138\)

RPython




Kode
# Sample size function for mean
n_for_mean <- function(sigma, e, alpha = 0.05, N_pop = Inf) {
  z <- qnorm(1 - alpha / 2)
  n0 <- (z^2 * sigma^2) / e^2
  if (is.finite(N_pop)) n0 / (1 + n0 / N_pop) else n0
}

# Grid
params <- expand_grid(
  sigma = c(1e6, 2e6, 3e6, 4e6),
  e = seq(1e5, 1e6, by = 5e4)
) |>
  mutate(
    n_inf = map2_dbl(sigma, e, ~n_for_mean(.x, .y)),
    n_fpc = map2_dbl(sigma, e, ~n_for_mean(.x, .y, N_pop = 10000)),
    sigma_label = paste0("σ = ", format(sigma, big.mark = ","))
  )

ggplot(params, aes(x = e, y = n_fpc, color = sigma_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title = "Sample Size vs Margin of Error (Estimasi Mean)",
    subtitle = "N = 10,000 | 95% confidence | dengan FPC",
    x = "Margin of Error (e)",
    y = "Required Sample Size (n)",
    color = NULL
  )








Gambar 1: Ukuran sampel yang diperlukan sebagai fungsi margin of error dan estimasi σ.









Kode
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
from scipy.stats import norm

def n_for_mean(sigma, e, alpha=0.05, N_pop=np.inf):
    z = norm.ppf(1 - alpha / 2)
    n0 = (z**2 * sigma**2) / e**2
    if np.isfinite(N_pop):
        return n0 / (1 + n0 / N_pop)
    return n0

sigmas = [1e6, 2e6, 3e6, 4e6]
e_vals = np.arange(1e5, 1.05e6, 5e4)
colors = ['#2563eb', '#059669', '#d97706', '#dc2626']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
for sigma, color in zip(sigmas, colors):
    n_vals = [n_for_mean(sigma, e, N_pop=10000) for e in e_vals]
    ax.plot(e_vals, n_vals, linewidth=1.5, color=color,
            label=f'σ = {sigma:,.0f}')

2 SRS untuk Estimasi Proporsi

2.1 Formula
\[
n = \frac{z^2 \cdot p(1-p)}{e^2}
\tag{3}\]
Dengan FPC: \(n = n_0 / (1 + n_0/N)\).






TipConservative vs Informed




Conservative (\(p = 0.5\)): menghasilkan \(n\) terbesar → aman untuk semua proporsi.
Informed prior (\(p\) dari studi sebelumnya): menghasilkan \(n\) lebih kecil → lebih efisien jika prior akurat.

Contoh: \(e = 3\%\), 95% CI. Conservative: \(n = 1.96^2 \times 0.25 / 0.03^2 = 1{,}068\). Jika \(p = 0.10\): \(n = 1.96^2 \times 0.09 / 0.03^2 = 384\).

text:
3 Perbandingan Dua Mean / Dua Proporsi

3.1 Dua Independent Means
\[
n_{\text{per group}} = \frac{(z_{1-\alpha/2} + z_{1-\beta})^2 \cdot 2\sigma^2}{\delta^2}
\tag{4}\]
di mana \(\delta = \mu_1 - \mu_2\) adalah perbedaan yang ingin dideteksi.


3.2 Dua Independent Proportions
\[
n_{\text{per group}} = \frac{(z_{1-\alpha/2} + z_{1-\beta})^2 \cdot [p_1(1-p_1) + p_2(1-p_2)]}{(p_1 - p_2)^2}
\tag{5}\]

title: Penentuan Ukuran Sampel
text:
4 Adjustment Factors

4.1 Non-Response Adjustment
\[
n_{adj} = \frac{n}{1 - r}
\tag{6}\]
di mana \(r\) = expected non-response rate. Jika \(n = 400\) dan \(r = 0.20\): \(n_{adj} = 400 / 0.80 = 500\).


4.2 Design Effect Adjustment
\[
n_{eff} = n_{\text{SRS}} \times \text{DEFF}
\tag{7}\]


4.3 Combined Adjustment
\[
n_{final} = \frac{n_{\text{SRS}} \times \text{DEFF}}{1 - r}
\tag{8}\]






PeringatanJangan Lupa Adjustment!



Rumus dasar mengasumsikan SRS dan 100% response rate. Dalam praktik, DEFF = 2–3 dan non-response = 20–40% sangat umum. Sampel 400 (rumus dasar) bisa menjadi \(400 \times 2.5 / 0.70 = 1{,}429\) setelah adjustment.

title: Penentuan Ukuran Sampel
text:
5 Sample Size untuk Stratified Sampling
Untuk alokasi proporsional:
\[
n = \frac{z^2 \sum W_h S_h^2}{e^2 + \frac{z^2}{N} \sum W_h S_h^2}
\]
Untuk alokasi Neyman, \(n\) lebih kecil karena alokasi lebih efisien — tetapi butuh informasi \(S_h\) yang akurat.

title: Penentuan Ukuran Sampel

6 Sample Size untuk Cluster Sampling
\[
n = n_{\text{SRS}} \times [1 + (\bar{m} - 1)\rho]
\tag{9}\]

PeringatanKritik Cohen’s Conventions



Cohen’s conventions adalah benchmarks generik — bukan pengganti effect size yang context-specific. “Small” effect di clinical trial bisa sangat bermakna (obat menyelamatkan nyawa). “Large” effect di social science bisa trivial. Selalu tentukan effect size berdasarkan:

Literature review pada domain yang sama

text:
8 Sample Size Calculator
Fungsi komprehensif untuk menghitung ukuran sampel dengan berbagai adjustment:

RPython



#' Comprehensive Sample Size Calculator
#'
#' @param type "mean" atau "proportion"
#' @param sigma SD populasi (untuk type = "mean")
#' @param p Expected proportion (untuk type = "proportion")
#' @param e Margin of error
#' @param alpha Significance level
#' @param N_pop Population size (Inf untuk infinite)
#' @param deff Design effect
#' @param response_rate Expected response rate (0 to 1)
#' @param n_subgroups Jumlah subgroup yang perlu dianalisis
calc_sample_size <- function(type = c("mean", "proportion"),
                              sigma = NULL, p = NULL,
                              e, alpha = 0.05,
                              N_pop = Inf, deff = 1,
                              response_rate = 1,
                              n_subgroups = 1) {
  type <- match.arg(type)
  z <- qnorm(1 - alpha / 2)

  if (type == "mean") {
    stopifnot(!is.null(sigma))
    n0 <- (z^2 * sigma^2) / e^2
  } else {
    if (is.null(p)) p <- 0.5
    n0 <- (z^2 * p * (1 - p)) / e^2
  }

  # FPC
  if (is.finite(N_pop)) {
    n0 <- n0 / (1 + n0 / N_pop)
  }

  # DEFF
  n_adj <- n0 * deff

  # Subgroups (ensure each subgroup has sufficient n)
  n_adj <- n_adj * n_subgroups

  # Non-response
  n_final <- ceiling(n_adj / response_rate)

  tibble(
    `n (basic)` = ceiling(n0),
    `n (with DEFF)` = ceiling(n0 * deff),
    `n (with subgroups)` = ceiling(n0 * deff * n_subgroups),
    `n (final, with NR adj)` = n_final,
    DEFF = deff,
    `Response Rate` = response_rate,
    `Subgroups` = n_subgroups
  )
}

# Example usage
calc_sample_size(
  type = "mean",
  sigma = 3000000,
  e = 500000,
  N_pop = 10000,
  deff = 2.0,
  response_rate = 0.75,
  n_subgroups = 1
) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Parameter", values_to = "Value") |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title = "Sample Size Calculation",
    subtitle = "Mean income | e = 500K | σ = 3M | DEFF = 2.0 | RR = 75%"
  )

import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import norm
import pandas as pd

def calc_sample_size(
    type='mean', sigma=None, p=None, e=None,
    alpha=0.05, N_pop=np.inf, deff=1,
    response_rate=1, n_subgroups=1
):
    z = norm.ppf(1 - alpha / 2)

    if type == 'mean':
        n0 = (z**2 * sigma**2) / e**2
    else:
        if p is None: p = 0.5
        n0 = (z**2 * p * (1 - p)) / e**2

    if np.isfinite(N_pop):
        n0 = n0 / (1 + n0 / N_pop)

    n_adj = n0 * deff * n_subgroups
    n_final = int(np.ceil(n_adj / response_rate))

    return pd.DataFrame({
        'Parameter': ['n (basic)', 'n (with DEFF)',
                      'n (final, with NR adj)', 'DEFF', 'Response Rate'],
        'Value': [int(np.ceil(n0)), int(np.ceil(n0 * deff)),
                  n_final, deff, response_rate]
    })

calc_sample_size(
    type='mean', sigma=3e6, e=5e5,
    N_pop=10000, deff=2.0, response_rate=0.75
)

                Parameter   Value
0               n (basic)  137.00
1           n (with DEFF)  273.00
2  n (final, with NR adj)  364.00
3                    DEFF    2.00
4           Response Rate    0.75

10 Bayesian Sample Size Determination

10.1 Average Posterior Variance Criterion (APVC)
Pilih \(n\) yang meminimalkan expected posterior variance:
\[
n^* = \arg\min_n \; E_y\left[\text{Var}(\theta \mid y)\right]
\]
Untuk normal model dengan prior \(\theta \sim N(\mu_0, \tau^2)\) dan data \(y \sim N(\theta, \sigma^2/n)\):
\[
\text{Var}(\theta \mid y) = \frac{1}{1/\tau^2 + n/\sigma^2}
\tag{11}\]


10.2 Average Coverage Criterion (ACC)
Pilih \(n\) sehingga posterior credible interval memiliki average coverage ≥ target:
\[
n^* = \min\{n : E_y[P(\theta \in \text{CI}_{1-\alpha} \mid y)] \geq 1 - \alpha\}
\]


10.3 Perbandingan Frequentist vs Bayesian








Aspek
Frequentist
Bayesian




Informasi prior
Tidak digunakan
Diinkorporasi via prior


Kriteria
MoE, power
Posterior variance, coverage


Interpretasi
Long-run frequency
Direct probability


Sensitivity
Terhadap σ, effect size
Terhadap prior + σ


Advantage
Simpler, well-understood
Can incorporate prior knowledge

# Frequentist: SE^2

df_compare <- tibble(
  n = rep(n_range, 2),
  variance = c(post_var, freq_var),
  approach = rep(c("Bayesian (τ=5M)", "Frequentist"), each = length(n_range))

  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +

    subtitle = "σ = 3M | Bayesian prior τ = 5M (diffuse)",

11 Sample Size untuk Machine Learning

11.1 Learning Curves Approach
Alih-alih formula, gunakan learning curves — plot performance metric vs training set size:

Train model pada subset data dengan ukuran berbeda: \(n_1 < n_2 < \ldots < n_k\)
Evaluate pada hold-out test set
Fit inverse power law: \(\text{Error}(n) = an^{-b} + c\)
Extrapolate untuk menentukan \(n\) yang mencapai target performance


RPython




Kode
set.seed(2024)

# Simulate learning curve data
n_train_sizes <- c(50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 5000, 7000)

# Simulate: error = a * n^(-b) + c + noise
a <- 5; b <- 0.5; c <- 0.15
lc_data <- tibble(
  n_train = n_train_sizes,
  error = a * n_train_sizes^(-b) + c + rnorm(length(n_train_sizes), 0, 0.01)
)

# Fit power law
fit <- nls(error ~ a * n_train^(-b) + c, data = lc_data,
           start = list(a = 5, b = 0.5, c = 0.15))

# Predict
n_pred <- seq(50, 10000, by = 50)
pred_error <- predict(fit, newdata = tibble(n_train = n_pred))

target_error <- 0.20
n_for_target <- n_pred[which.min(abs(pred_error - target_error))]

ggplot(lc_data, aes(x = n_train, y = error)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#2563eb") +
  geom_line(data = tibble(n_train = n_pred, error = pred_error),
            color = "#dc2626", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = target_error, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "#059669") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = n_for_target, linetype = "dotted",
             color = "#059669") +
  annotate("text", x = n_for_target + 500, y = target_error + 0.01,
           label = sprintf("n ≈ %d for error = %.2f", n_for_target, target_error),
           color = "#059669", size = 3.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_comma()) +
  labs(
    title = "Learning Curve: Sample Size for ML",
    subtitle = "Fitted inverse power law: Error = a·n^(-b) + c",
    x = "Training Set Size",
    y = "Test Error (e.g., 1 - Accuracy)"
  )

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.optimize import curve_fit

np.random.seed(2024)

n_sizes = np.array([50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 3000, 5000, 7000])
errors = 5 * n_sizes**(-0.5) + 0.15 + np.random.normal(0, 0.01, len(n_sizes))

def power_law(n, a, b, c):
    return a * n**(-b) + c

popt, _ = curve_fit(power_law, n_sizes, errors, p0=[5, 0.5, 0.15])

n_pred = np.arange(50, 10001, 50)
pred_errors = power_law(n_pred, *popt)

target = 0.20
n_target = n_pred[np.argmin(np.abs(pred_errors - target))]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
ax.scatter(n_sizes, errors, s=60, color='#2563eb', zorder=5)

Gambar 12: Learning curve approach for ML sample size (Python).










11.2 Key Takeaways

Formula dasar adalah starting point — selalu adjust untuk DEFF, non-response, dan subgroup analysis.
Effect size harus context-driven, bukan mengikuti Cohen’s conventions secara membabi-buta.
Power analysis membutuhkan empat parameter (\(\alpha\), power, effect size, \(n\)) — fix tiga, solve satu.
Bayesian sample size memanfaatkan prior information dan sering menghasilkan \(n\) lebih kecil jika prior informatif.
Untuk ML, gunakan learning curves daripada formula — lebih realistis dan data-driven.



11.3 Exercises

Multi-objective. Survei ingin mengestimasi (a) mean income dengan MoE Rp 300K, (b) proporsi internet access dengan MoE 2%, (c) mean expenditure per region (5 region). Hitung \(n\) final jika DEFF = 2.0 dan expected response rate = 70%.
Power analysis. Program intervensi diharapkan meningkatkan skor kepuasan dari 3.2 ke 3.5 (SD = 1.0). Berapa ukuran sampel per group (treatment vs control) untuk mendeteksi perbedaan ini dengan power 80% dan 90%? Plot power curve.
Learning curve. Gunakan dataset populasi untuk melatih model regresi (income ~ features). Buat learning curve dengan 10 titik training size. Estimasi berapa \(n\) yang dibutuhkan untuk mencapai R² = 0.60.



11.4 Further Reading

Cohen (1988): Referensi klasik power analysis.
Adcock (1997): Review komprehensif metode sample size determination.
Figueroa dkk. (2012): Sample size untuk classification problems.
